items.headline: US Equities Slide as Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Fears Mount
items.summary: U.S. equities closed lower on Friday, marking what could be the longest weekly losing streak in nearly four years, as escalating geopolitical tensions surrounding the Iran conflict and rising oil prices dampened investor sentiment. The Nasdaq Composite officially entered correction territory, dropping more than 10% from its October peak. Consumer sentiment also saw a significant decline in March, driven by concerns over higher gasoline prices and volatile financial markets. Crude oil benchmarks, Brent and WTI, continued their upward trajectory, topping $104 and $97 per barrel respectively.

items.headline: Valero Energy Surges on Price Target Hikes
items.summary: Valero Energy ($VLO) jumped 5.80% following fresh price target increases from Raymond James and BofA.

items.headline: Occidental Petroleum Rises Amid CEO Retirement Reports
items.summary: Occidental Petroleum ($OXY) added 4.06% after reports indicated CEO Vicki Hollub is preparing to retire.

items.headline: CF Industries Climbs After UBS Price Target Increase
items.summary: CF Industries Holdings ($CF) climbed 3.47% after UBS raised its price target on the fertilizer producer.

items.headline: NVIDIA Leads Dow Losers
items.summary: NVIDIA Corporation ($NVDA) was the Dow's major loser, with its stock price falling 4.2%.

items.headline: Lumentum Holdings Drops on New Facility Investment News
items.summary: Lumentum Holdings ($LITE) dropped 11.37% after announcing a major new U.S. manufacturing facility investment.

items.headline: Ciena Falls Despite Analyst Upgrade
items.summary: Ciena ($CIEN) fell 11.36% despite a recent price target increase from Stifel.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Dips as Iran War Fuels Inflation Concerns
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment dropped to 53.3 in March, the lowest level since December 2025, primarily due to the ongoing Iran conflict and the resultant surge in gas prices. Americans' inflation expectations for the next 12 months rose to 3.8% from 3.4% in February, marking the largest one-month increase in nearly a year.

items.headline: Fed's Barkin Highlights Iran War's Complications on Economic Outlook
items.summary: Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin stated that the Iran war has significantly complicated the economic outlook, posing risks to both inflation and consumer demand. He noted that holding interest rates steady in March was a prudent decision given the elevated uncertainty.

items.headline: Oil Prices Climb Amid Continued Strait of Hormuz Closure and Geopolitical Uncertainty
items.summary: Despite President Trump's repeated extensions of the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the waterway remains effectively closed, contributing to a sustained rise in global oil prices. Brent crude is trading above $104 per barrel, and WTI around $97 per barrel, significantly higher than pre-conflict levels. This geopolitical instability remains a dominant market driver.

items.headline: Energy Sector Shows Resilience, Tech and Industrials Lag
items.summary: While the broader market faced declines, the Energy sector demonstrated resilience with gains. In contrast, the Communication Services, Information Technology, and Industrials sectors were among the day's biggest decliners. Metals and mining stocks have experienced substantial losses since the onset of the Iran war.

items.headline: Major Companies Report Earnings, Analyst Ratings Updated
items.summary: Carnival ($CCL), Kopin ($KOPN), Autolus Therapeutics ($AUTL), TMC the metals company Inc. ($TMC), Humacyte ($HUMA), and Legence Corp. Class A ($LGN) are among the companies expected to report earnings today. Analysts at Wedbush reiterated an outperform rating on Apple ($AAPL), while Wells Fargo maintained an overweight rating on Nvidia ($NVDA) and Alphabet ($GOOG).

items.headline: China Initiates Retaliatory Trade Probes Against U.S.
items.summary: Adding to market jitters, China's Commerce Ministry launched two retaliatory trade probes against the U.S. This move comes in response to earlier U.S. trade investigations and could heighten tensions between the two economic powers.
